# Spec context: §6.1 — Typesetting

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#typesetting>

- Section: 6. Typesetting and playback
- Subsection: 6.1 Typesetting

## Preceding prose

> * "`I:linebreak !`" indicates that the `!` symbol is used to typeset a [score line-break](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"). Any [code line-breaks](#code_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") are ignored for typesetting purposes.
> *Comment:* The "`I:linebreak !`" instruction works in the same way as `I:linebreak $` and is primarily provided for backwards compatibility - see [line-breaking dialects](#line-breaking_dialects "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"), so that "`I:linebreak $`" is the preferred usage. "`I:linebreak !`" also automati
> * "`I:linebreak `" indicates that the End Of Line character (CR, LF or CRLF) is used to typeset a [score line-break](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵"). In other words, [code line-breaks](#code_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") are used for typesetting [score line-breaks]
> * "`I:linebreak `" indicates that all line-breaking is to be carried out automatically and any [code line-breaks](#code_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") are ignored for typesetting purposes.
> The values ``, `$` and `!` may also be combined so that more that one symbol can indicate a [score line-break](#score_line-break_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵").
> The default line-break setting is:
